activity_title: Existing leakage framework and population-method investigation
activity_summary: Confirmed the development leakage tables and the PH action board are populated by an external daily batch - created_at clusters near 08:00 with dated detection_run_id values - and that there is no in-database trigger, function or pg_cron scheduler. Identified the downstream consumers (priority engine, kill-timer status, top-10 leakage and the PH action board). Recorded that detections remain OPEN with zero closures.
evidence_refs: information_schema.triggers (empty); pg_proc (no writers); pg_extension (no cron); detection_run_id timestamps
validation_rule: The populator must be shown from real metadata or proven absent
gap_or_risk: The leakage tables are externally owned - any WLSP write needs a run_id and analysis_type convention agreed with the external ETL owner
next_action: Coordinate run_id and analysis_type convention with the external ETL owner before any persistence

activity_title: L1-L5 coverage and gap-size determination versus the existing framework
activity_summary: Determined the existing framework supports zero analyses for reuse without modification, L2 is reusable with minor modification because the 25 percent threshold already matches, and L1, L3, L4 and L5 require new calculations. Confirmed the leakage tables are a derived summary and lifecycle layer that holds no PPC, no raw revenue, no raw shipping cost and no refund-count fields, so they cannot serve as the calculation source.
evidence_refs: leakage_detection column inventory (no ppc/spend); leakage_classification cost-percent fields; COVERAGE_MATRIX.md
validation_rule: Each coverage verdict must cite a field-level fact
gap_or_risk: L2 is the only direct rule match; all other analyses must be calculated from source tables
next_action: Calculate L1-L5 directly from source tables

activity_title: WLSP source-data validation across the four source tables
activity_summary: Profiled ppc_performance, order_transaction, order_shipping_billing_detail and amazon_returns for row counts, date coverage, key-field nulls and ASIN, SKU and PH availability. Found all five analyses calculable directly from source with readiness about 85 to 95 percent and no hard blockers. Produced the source-data readiness matrix, the missing-field report and the blocker report.
evidence_refs: source-table profile queries (read-only); SOURCE_DATA_READINESS_MATRIX; MISSING_FIELD_REPORT; BLOCKER_REPORT
validation_rule: Each analysis must return data when scoped to its protocol window
gap_or_risk: DQ-1 PPC PH attribution and DQ-2 refund reconciliation remain known limitations but are not hard blockers; PPC queries must be window-scoped to avoid timeout

activity_summary: Authored CALCULATION_DESIGN.md specifying for each analysis the source tables, source columns, exact formula, filters, date window, join logic, grouping level, output columns, PH assignment method and data-quality risks. Flagged that shipping must be de-duplicated per order_id before any ASIN ratio and that PPC must be window-scoped. Recommended a hybrid - render the report from source on demand and persist only the flagged set into the existing lifecycle for closure.
reusable_pattern: Source-query calculation-design template per analysis - tables, columns, formula, filters, window, join, grain, output, PH method and DQ risks
validation_rule: Every analysis must define all ten design elements and a justified store-vs-generate decision
gap_or_risk: Pure on-demand cannot support the Monday verification; any persistence is gated on OPTION B sign-off
next_action: Implement L1 first at ASIN level on OPTION B sign-off

activity_title: Closure-lifecycle capability audit of the existing PH workflow
activity_summary: Verified the existing workflow already models the full closure lifecycle - the development.actions spine carries status, completed_date, evidence and economics and is guarded by a BEFORE UPDATE trigger that enforces evidence on close; an 11-state status taxonomy already includes VERIFIED, CLOSED and PREVENTION_CONFIRMED; a verification table records the validator and before/after metrics; and a verification schedule of 82 rows carries day 3, 7, 15, 30 and 60 due dates for periodic verification. Concluded that no new WLSP tracking or storage tables are required.
evidence_refs: information_schema.columns and table_constraints; triggers (trg_enforce_evidence_on_close); ph_lifecycle_status_taxonomy_v1 (11 rows); ph_action_verification_schedule_v1 (82 rows)
validation_rule: Reuse the existing lifecycle - do not create a parallel tracking table
gap_or_risk: The lifecycle tables are near-empty which is a maturity gap - the capability exists but has not yet been exercised end to end
next_action: Exercise the existing lifecycle rather than build new storage
